stem: The CPU processes instructions using the fetch–decode–execute cycle.
model_answer: Fetch: the CPU copies the next instruction from main memory into the CPU using the address held in the program counter, then increments the program counter. Decode: the Control Unit interprets the instruction and identifies what operation is required and which data is needed. Execute: the CPU (using the ALU where appropriate) carries out the instruction, reading or writing memory or registers as required.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Describe the three stages of the fetch–decode–execute cycle.

mark_points.text: fetch: the next instruction is copied from main memory into the CPU (using the program counter, which is then incremented)
accepted_alternatives: fetch: instruction is read from RAM
fetch: CPU retrieves the instruction at the address in the PC

mark_points.text: decode: the Control Unit interprets the instruction and works out what is required
execute: the CPU carries out the instruction (using the ALU for arithmetic or logic)
accepted_alternatives: execute: ALU carries out the operation
